mechanism_narrative: Elevated federal funds rate levels increase the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show positive mean or Rank IC > 0.05 when FEDFUNDS exceeds 4.5%.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid quarterly increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.5 percentage points signals an abrupt deterioration in labor market conditions, triggering recession fears that drive negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: Forward 60-day returns remain positive in >55% of instances where UNRATE crosses the 0.5pp threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in real sales of automobiles and housing starts indicate a synchronized collapse in consumer durables demand, which reduces corporate revenue expectations and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Joint negative momentum in RSAFS and HOUST fails to predict negative 20-day returns with statistical significance (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Positive month-over-month growth in the index of industrial production reflects expanding manufacturing capacity utilization, which generates short-term positive S&P 500 returns when the economy operates in a stable growth regime without monetary tightening.
failure_condition: INDPRO positive growth yields negative or flat 5-day forward returns during stable growth periods, or Rank IC < 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained high consumer price index levels erode real household purchasing power and force central banks to maintain restrictive policy, creating a headwind that produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Quarterly forward returns are positive or neutral when CPIAUCSL exceeds 205, or the signal shows no predictive power in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: A single-month drop in nonfarm payrolls exceeding 100,000 signals an immediate shock to aggregate income, triggering rapid risk-off positioning that drives negative S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in >60% of instances following a >100k payroll decline, or the threshold effect vanishes after controlling for prior trend.

mechanism_narrative: The combination of slowing real GDP growth and elevated federal funds rates creates a policy mismatch that suppresses corporate profit margins, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The interaction term between GDPC1 growth and FEDFUNDS levels shows no incremental predictive power over single-series models in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising industrial production and stable unemployment indicates improving labor productivity, which expands profit margins and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter during recovery phases.
failure_condition: The productivity divergence signal fails to predict positive 60-day returns during recovery regimes, or the joint effect is statistically indistinguishable from zero.
